perturbation.original_text: Within 7 days of a written request by the Discloser, the Recipient must return all Confidential Information, and provide a signed certificate stating that al l Confidential Information in the Recipient's possession or control has been returned.
perturbation.changed_text: Within 7 days of a written request by the Discloser, the Recipient may, at their discretion, return all Confidential Information, and provide a signed certificate stating that all Confidential Information in the Recipient's possession or control has been returned, or provide a reason for not returning the information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an 'In-Text Contradiction' by making the return of Confidential Information discretionary for the Recipient, which contradicts the earlier clause 4, which states that the Recipient 'must ensure that Confidential Information is maintained confidential with at least the standard of care that the Recipient maintains to protect its own confidential information'. By adding 'at their discretion' and giving the option of providing a reason for non-return, it undermines the mandatory obligation to return all confidential information, thus creating uncertainty about enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION OBLIGATIONS In consideration of each party disclosing its Confidential Information to the other party for the Purpose, the Recipient must ensure that Confidential Information is:  maintained confidential with at least the standard of care that the Recipient maintains t o protect its own confidential information;  not disclosed to any Unauthorised Person;  only used for the Purpose; and  not reproduced in any form except as required for the purpose. The Recipient must ensure that access to Confidential Information is only given to those of its officers, employees and advisers who require access for the Purpose, and that those officers, employees and advisers are informed of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information an d kee p that information confidential. Parties to whom the information is imparted must be warned that include d ‘insider information ,’ regarding publicly listed and traded shares may preclud e them from such trade in those shares . The Confidential Informatio n remains at all times the property of the Discloser, and other than as provided in this agreement, no rights in respect of the Confidential Information are granted or conveyed to the Recipient. Confidentiality & Non -Disclosure Agreement ICT Strategic Consulting 2 of 2 Ph: +61 (02) 9591 1464 56 Minnamorra Avenue Earlwood NSW 2206 Australia www.ICTStrategicConsulting.com In the event that the Recipient is legally required to disclo se any Confidential Information, the Recipient must immediately notify the Discloser of that fact. Within 7 days of a written request by the Discloser, the Recipient may, at their discretion, return all Confidential Information, and provide a signed certificate stating that all Confidential Information in the Recipient's possession or control has been returned, or provide a reason for not returning the information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Recipient 'must return' Confidential Information. The changed text modifies this to 'may, at their discretion, return,' effectively making the return optional. This directly contradicts the 'Confidential Information Obligations' section in the Contradicted Text, which states that the Recipient 'must ensure' confidentiality. Making the return discretionary undermines this obligation and creates a direct conflict within the document, satisfying the definition of a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement is governed by the laws of New South Wales, Australia.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement is governed by the laws of New South Wales, Australia, unless the confidential information relates to activities outside of Australia, in which case the laws of that jurisdiction will apply.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an 'In-Text Contradiction' by initially stating that the agreement is governed by the laws of New South Wales, Australia. However, the added clause introduces a condition where the governing law changes based on the geographical location of activities related to the confidential information. This undermines the original statement by creating uncertainty about which jurisdiction's laws apply, and could cause legal disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: GENERAL The parties make no warranties or representations to each other in relation to any Confidential Information including (without limitation) as to the accur acy or completeness of any Confidential Information. A power or right in this agreement may only be waived in writing, signed by the party to be bound by the waiver, and this agreement may only be amended or supplemented in writing executed by both parties . The obligations in this agreement survive the termination or purported termination of this agreement. This agreement is governed by the laws of New South Wales, Australia, unless the confidential information relates to activities outside of Australia, in which case the laws of that jurisdiction will apply. The parties submit to the non -exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of that State.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a condition that the governing law can change based on the location of confidential information activities. This directly contradicts the original statement that the agreement is governed by the laws of New South Wales, Australia. The contradicted text also states that this agreement is governed by the laws of New South Wales, Australia, unless the confidential information relates to activities outside of Australia, in which case the laws of that jurisdiction will apply which introduces an 'In-Text Contradiction' by initially stating that the agreement is governed by the laws of New South Wales, Australia. However, the added clause introduces a condition where the governing law changes based on the geographical location of activities related to the confidential information. This undermines the original statement by creating uncertainty about which jurisdiction's laws apply. This falls under Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction as a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: all information treated by the Discloser as confidential
perturbation.changed_text: all information that the Discloser verbally indicates is confidential.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an 'In-Text Contradiction'. Previously, information was simply ‘treated’ as confidential, implying a broader scope of what could be considered confidential. Now it is limited to what is verbally indicated, it is now contradicting with the rest of the contract, that there could be a lot of information that could be consider confidential, apart from what is verbally communicated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATIONS "Confidential Information " of a disclosing party (" Discloser ") means the following , regardless of its form and including copies made by the receiving party (" Recipient "), whether the Recipient becomes aware of it before or after the date of this Agreement:  all information that the Discloser verbally indicates is confidential; and  disclosed by the Discloser to the Recipient or o f which the Recipient becomes aware, including but not limited to, the information specified in Schedule 1; except where that information is:  made public other than by the Reci pient breaching this agreement ;  legally required to be disclose d by the Recipie nt;  known to the Recipient free of any obligation to keep it confidential; or  Independently developed by the Recipient without use, directly or indirectly of Confidential Information received from the Discloser. "Unauthorised Persons " means officers, empl oyees or advisers of the Recipient who are not required to have access to the Confidential Information for the Purpose, and any other person who is not a party to this agreement. References to clauses and parties are references to clauses of, and parties t o, this agreement; references to things include parts of those things; references to persons include legal and natural persons; and words importing the singular include the plural and vice versa.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only include what is 'verbally indicated'. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information' more broadly, including 'all information that the Discloser verbally indicates is confidential; and disclosed by the Discloser to the Recipient or o f which the Recipient becomes aware'. By limiting the definition to only verbally indicated information, the changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text, which includes other forms of disclosed information as confidential. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient must ensure that access to Confidential Information is only given to those of its officers, employees and advisers who require access for the Purpose, and that those officers, employees and advisers are informed of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information an d kee p that information confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient may provide access to Confidential Information to its officers, employees, and advisors, regardless of whether they require access for the Purpose. While informing them of the confidential nature of the information is encouraged, it is not mandatory.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that access to Confidential Information is restricted to those who require it for the Purpose and that they must be informed of its confidential nature. The modified text directly contradicts this by stating that access may be granted regardless of the Purpose and that informing them of confidentiality is not mandatory. This creates a significant 'In-Text Contradiction' regarding the protection of confidential information. The use of 'may' and 'encouraged, but not mandatory' directly opposes the previous requirement of 'must ensure'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION OBLIGATIONS In consideration of each party disclosing its Confidential Information to the other party for the Purpose, the Recipient must ensure that Confidential Information is:  maintained confidential with at least the standard of care that the Recipient maintains t o protect its own confidential information;  not disclosed to any Unauthorised Person;  only used for the Purpose; and  not reproduced in any form except as required for the purpose. The Recipient may provide access to Confidential Information to its officers, employees, and advisors, regardless of whether they require access for the Purpose. While informing them of the confidential nature of the information is encouraged, it is not mandatory. Parties to whom the information is imparted must be warned that include d ‘insider information ,’ regarding publicly listed and traded shares may preclud e them from such trade in those shares . The Confidential Informatio n remains at all times the property of the Discloser, and other than as provided in this agreement, no rights in respect of the Confidential Information are granted or conveyed to the Recipient. Confidentiality & Non -Disclosure Agreement ICT Strategic Consulting 2 of 2 Ph: +61 (02) 9591 1464 56 Minnamorra Avenue Earlwood NSW 2206 Australia www.ICTStrategicConsulting.com In the event that the Recipient is legally required to disclo se any Confidential Information, the Recipient must immediately notify the Discloser of that fact. Within 7 days of a written request by the Discloser, the Recipient must return all Confidential Information, and provide a signed certificate stating that al l Confidential Information in the Recipient's possession or control has been returned.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The changed text states that access to Confidential Information 'may' be provided 'regardless of whether they require access for the Purpose' and that informing them of the confidential nature of the information is 'encouraged, but not mandatory'. This directly opposes the contradicted text's requirement that 'the Recipient must ensure that Confidential Information is: ... only used for the Purpose' and maintained confidentially.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information " of a disclosing party (" Discloser ") means the following , regardless of its form and including copies made by the receiving party (" Recipient "), whether the Recipient becomes aware of it before or after the date of this Agreement
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information " of a disclosing party (" Discloser ") means the following , only if the Discloser provides written confirmation that the specific information is to be treated as confidential, and only including the original document, but not copies.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an 'In-Text Contradiction'. The original text defines Confidential Information broadly, encompassing all information regardless of form and timing of awareness. The modification introduces restrictions: 1) requiring written confirmation from the Discloser and 2) excluding copies. This new definition contradicts the initial broader definition by now requiring confirmation that the specific information is to be treated as confidential, and only including the original document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATIONS "Confidential Information " of a disclosing party (" Discloser ") means the following , only if the Discloser provides written confirmation that the specific information is to be treated as confidential, and only including the original document, but not copies:  all information treated by the Discloser as confidential; and  disclosed by the Discloser to the Recipient or o f which the Recipient becomes aware, including but not limited to, the information specified in Schedule 1; except where that information is:  made public other than by the Reci pient breaching this agreement ;  legally required to be disclose d by the Recipie nt;  known to the Recipient free of any obligation to keep it confidential; or  Independently developed by the Recipient without use, directly or indirectly of Confidential Information received from the Discloser. "Unauthorised Persons " means officers, empl oyees or advisers of the Recipient who are not required to have access to the Confidential Information for the Purpose, and any other person who is not a party to this agreement. References to clauses and parties are references to clauses of, and parties t o, this agreement; references to things include parts of those things; references to persons include legal and natural persons; and words importing the singular include the plural and vice versa.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' to require written confirmation and exclude copies, which directly conflicts with the contradicted text which defines 'Confidential Information' the same way as the changed text. The changed text does therefore cause a contradiction, as it defines the same term twice in the same document but with conflicting implications, as noted in the explanation.
